Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The IBM scientist who rewrote the rules of information just won a Turing Award 106 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Teaching Claude to QA a mobile app 70 upvotes, 6 comments
  3. How to Attract AI Bots to Your Open Source Project 88 upvotes, 13 comments
  4. "Collaboration" Is Bullshit 21 upvotes, 1 comments
  5. Building an FPGA 3dfx Voodoo with Modern RTL Tools 165 upvotes, 34 comments
  6. GrapheneOS will remain usable by anyone without requiring personal information 256 upvotes, 69 comments
  7. 25 Years of Eggs 256 upvotes, 73 comments
  8. Project Nomad – Knowledge That Never Goes Offline 374 upvotes, 113 comments
  9. Intuitions for Tranformer Circuits 20 upvotes, 2 comments
  10. Five Years of Running a Systems Reading Group at Microsoft 123 upvotes, 35 comments
  11. Ordered Dithering with Arbitrary or Irregular Colour Palettes (2023) 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  12. Flash-MoE: Running a 397B Parameter Model on a Laptop 312 upvotes, 107 comments
  13. The gold standard of optimization: A look under the hood of RollerCoaster Tycoon 229 upvotes, 78 comments
  14. Migrating the American Express Payment Network, Twice 16 upvotes, 2 comments
  15. More common mistakes to avoid when creating system architecture diagrams 143 upvotes, 52 comments
  16. I Reverse-Engineered the TiinyAI Pocket Lab from Marketing Photos 36 upvotes, 9 comments
  17. Theodosian Land Walls of Constantinople (2025) 29 upvotes, 7 comments
  18. PC Gamer recommends RSS readers in a 37mb article that just keeps downloading 367 upvotes, 172 comments
  19. MAUI Is Coming to Linux 170 upvotes, 81 comments
  20. LLMs predict my coffee 82 upvotes, 37 comments
  21. The future of version control 433 upvotes, 248 comments
  22. First and Lego Education Partnership Update 29 upvotes, 11 comments
  23. Why I love NixOS 223 upvotes, 153 comments
  24. Show HN: Codala, a social network built on scanning barcodes 34 upvotes, 17 comments
  25. Reports of code's death are greatly exaggerated 283 upvotes, 223 comments
  26. They're Vibe-Coding Spam Now 42 upvotes, 28 comments
  27. Windows native app development is a mess 347 upvotes, 355 comments
  28. What Young Workers Are Doing to AI-Proof Themselves 82 upvotes, 103 comments
  29. The hottest new phone is Tin Can, a 'landline' for kids 15 upvotes, 12 comments
  30. You are not your job 79 upvotes, 105 comments

Last generated 11:00am, 23 March 2026. Source here.
